channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canberra United’s A-League Season Ends in Heartbreak Amid Uncertainty Over Future
item.description: Canberra United’s A-League finals hopes came to a dramatic end with a 3-1 loss to Melbourne Victory, leaving the future of the club uncertain. Despite a strong performance from homegrown talent Sasha ...

item.title: Brumbies defeated by Hurricanes at new Christchurch stadium
item.description: The ACT Brumbies suffered a heavy 45-12 defeat against the Hurricanes at Christchurch's new Te Kaha Stadium, a venue designed to withstand major earthquakes. Despite the Brumbies' hopes of hosting a h...

item.title: Palestinian Elections Held Despite Israeli Restrictions
item.description: Palestinians recently held elections, marking a significant moment for the region despite facing considerable challenges and restrictions imposed by Israel. The vote, considered a landmark event, aims...

item.title: Wine investment opportunities as market recovers: expert picks bottles with growth potential
item.description: The article explains that the fine wine market has experienced a significant downturn following a pandemic-era boom, during which demand and prices surged. Over the past three years, prices declined d...

item.title: Army’s 10th Infantry Division takes over Task Force GenSan operations in General Santos
item.description: Inilipat na sa ilalim ng kontrol ng Philippine Army’s 10th Infantry Division ang Task Force GenSan, isang anti-terror unit na sumasaklaw sa General Santos City. Ang turnover ay isinagawa noong April 2...

item.title: Akwa Ibom Governor Marks Birthday with Welfare Support for Orphans and Caregivers
item.description: Akwa Ibom State Governor, Umo Eno, use him 62nd birthday take do something wey carry strong social impact, as he celebrate am with children for orphanages and announce different support measures for t...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi Criticises RSS After Ram Madhav’s Controversial Remarks on Oil Imports from Russia and Iran
item.description: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi launched a scathing attack on the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) following controversial remarks by senior BJP leader Ram Madhav. In a social media post, Gandhi referre...

item.title: Canadians Face Travel Chaos Under New EU Entry Rules
item.description: Recent changes to the European Union's entry requirements have caused significant delays and confusion for Canadian travellers. The new rules, which are part of an effort to better regulate immigratio...

item.title: Schoolchildren safely stop bus after driver loses consciousness
item.description: In Mississippi, a potentially tragic incident was narrowly avoided when a school bus driver lost consciousness while driving. Thanks to the quick thinking and courage of the schoolchildren on board, t...

item.title: Coffee industry partners roll out satellite and AI system to monitor deforestation in growing regions
item.description: Several major coffee companies and commodity traders have teamed up to launch a new satellite-driven system aimed at tracking deforestation linked to coffee farming across the globe. The initiative, k...

item.title: OpenAI CEO Apologizes for Not Reporting Tumbler Ridge Shooter’s Online Messages
item.description: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, issued a formal apology for the company’s failure to report warning signs in the online activity of Jesse Van Rootselaar, who committed a mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C...

item.title: PDEA-9 seizes over P54 million worth of shabu in two Zamboanga City buy-bust operations
item.description: Authorities from the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency in Region 9 (PDEA-9) successfully confiscated a total of P54.4 million worth of shabu in two separate anti-drug operations conducted in Zamboang...

item.title: LTFRB Fuel Subsidy Payout Sparks Tension Due to Queue Issues
item.description: Nagkagulo sa LTFRB (Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board) nitong Abril 25, 2026, nang magkakaroon ng payout para sa fuel subsidy na para sa mga taxi drivers. Dumami ang nag-aabang at n...

item.title: Man Accused of Robbing Travellers While Disguised as a Woman Arrested by Public, Sparks Debate on Mob Justice
item.description: There was drama after a young Kenyan man was caught by members of the public for allegedly robbing travellers at night while disguised as a woman. According to a video shared online, the suspect had r...

item.title: Critique of India’s Regional Policy and Narrative After Pahalgam
item.description: The article presents a strongly critical opinion on India’s political and foreign policy posture one year after the Pahalgam attack in occupied Kashmir. The writer argues that India, particularly unde...

item.title: US shifts diplomatic approach as Vance skips latest Iran talks in Pakistan
item.description: The United States is adjusting its diplomatic strategy in ongoing efforts to stabilise tensions with Iran, as Vice President JD Vance will not attend the next round of talks in Islamabad. According to...

item.title: Unexplained surge in arson attacks on Melbourne restaurants and bars
item.description: Since 14 April, at least eight restaurants, bars, and nightclubs in Melbourne have been targeted in a series of arson attacks, with the most recent incident involving the South Yarra restaurant France...

item.title: PWHL regular season ends with playoff spots, top seed, and draft order still undecided
item.description: The PWHL regular season wraps up with a decisive “Super Saturday,” where all eight teams play and multiple key outcomes remain unresolved. The biggest storyline centres on the final playoff berth, whi...

item.title: US considers increasing refugee intake focused on white South Africans amid policy shift
item.description: The Trump administration is reportedly considering expanding the United States refugee programme to admit more white South Africans, particularly Afrikaners, as part of a broader shift in immigration ...

item.title: Seven AAP MPs resign citing leadership issues, Raghav Chadha joins BJP
item.description: In a significant political development, seven Members of Parliament (MPs) from the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) have tendered their resignation, citing issues related to the party's leadership. The resignati...
